name: DANDY-WALKER MALFORMATION
definition: characterized CLASSICALLY by continuum of anomalies, including Dandy-Walker malformation + Dandy-Walker variant + developing normal rhombencephalon during 1st trimester

demographics.Path: defect in vermis connecting an ependyma-lined retrocerebellar cyst with 4th ventricle

discriminating_features: torcular-lambdoid inversion = upward displacement of tentorium + torcular herophili +

general: torcular-lambdoid inversion = upward displacement of tentorium + torcular herophili +
small widely separated ± hypoplastic cerebellar hemispheres ← anterolateral
large posterior fossa cyst with extension through foramen magnum = diverticulum of
cerebellar hemispheres in apposition without intervening vermis following shunt procedure
ventriculomegaly (in 72% open communication with 3rd ventricle; in 39% patent 4th
ANGIO: anterosuperiorly displaced superior cerebellar arteries above the posterior cerebral
small / absent PICA with high tonsillar loop
communication between retrocerebellar cyst and subarachnoid space through a patent
posterior fossa smaller than in usual Dandy-Walker syndrome
incomplete closure of vermis is normal until 18 weeks GA!
fluid-filled space in posterior aspect of fetal head

clinical.Cx: trapping of cyst above tentorium = "keyhole configuration"
clinical.Prognosis: fetal demise in 66%; 22-50% mortality during 1st year of life
